## Classes

### <a name="trocla--ca--params"></a>`trocla::ca::params`

Default values for generating an 509 CA

#### Examples

##### Generating an 509 CA

```puppet
trocla('some_ca', 'x509', $trocla::ca::params::ca_options)
```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `trocla::ca::params` class:

* [`trocla_options`](#-trocla--ca--params--trocla_options)

##### <a name="-trocla--ca--params--trocla_options"></a>`trocla_options`

Data type: `Hash`

Default trola options for a CA. This gets merged with defaut options that
indicate the certificate should be a CA. You can pass
`$trocla::ca::params::ca_options` as the third parameter to `trocla()` in
order to get an x509 CA.

Default value:

```puppet
{
    'profiles' => ['sysdomain_nc','x509veryverylong'],
    'CN'       => "automated-ca ${name} for ${facts['networking']['domain']}",
  }
```

### <a name="trocla--config"></a>`trocla::config`

Installs configuration files for the trocla agent/CLI

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `trocla::config` class:

* [`options`](#-trocla--config--options)
* [`profiles`](#-trocla--config--profiles)
* [`x509_profile_domain_constraints`](#-trocla--config--x509_profile_domain_constraints)
* [`store`](#-trocla--config--store)
* [`store_options`](#-trocla--config--store_options)
* [`encryption`](#-trocla--config--encryption)
* [`encryption_options`](#-trocla--config--encryption_options)
* [`manage_dependencies`](#-trocla--config--manage_dependencies)
* [`edit_uid`](#-trocla--config--edit_uid)

##### <a name="-trocla--config--options"></a>`options`

Data type: `Hash`

Options for trocla. Default: empty hash.

Default value: `{}`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--profiles"></a>`profiles`

Data type: `Hash`

Profiles for trocla. Default: empty hash.

Default value: `{}`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--x509_profile_domain_constraints"></a>`x509_profile_domain_constraints`

Data type: `Array[String]`

A profile for x509 name constraint that matches the own domain by default.
This will add a profile for x509 certs with the option 'name_constraints'
set to this array of domains.

Default value: `[$facts['networking']['domain']]`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--store"></a>`store`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Defines the store to be used for trocla. By default it's not set, meaning
trocla's default (moneta) will be used.

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--store_options"></a>`store_options`

Data type: `Hash`

This will contain a hash of the options to pass the trocla store
configuration.

Default value: `{}`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--encryption"></a>`encryption`

Data type: `Optional[Variant[String, Array[String]]]`

Defines the encryption method for password stored in the backend. By default
it's not set, meaning trocla's default (none) will be used.

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--encryption_options"></a>`encryption_options`

Data type: `Hash`

This will contain a hash of the options for the encryption. Default: empty
Hash

Default value: `{}`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--manage_dependencies"></a>`manage_dependencies`

Data type: `Boolean`

Whether to manage the dependencies or not. Default *true*

Default value: `true`

##### <a name="-trocla--config--edit_uid"></a>`edit_uid`

Data type: `String`

Name of the group assigned to the troclarc file.

Default value: `'puppet'`

### <a name="trocla--master"></a>`trocla::master`

Manage the necessary things for trocla on a master.

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `trocla::master` class:

* [`provider`](#-trocla--master--provider)

##### <a name="-trocla--master--provider"></a>`provider`

Data type: `String`

Name of the package provider used for installing the trocla package. The
default value ('default') uses the distro's package manager.

Default value: `'default'`

### <a name="trocla--master--hiera"></a>`trocla::master::hiera`

manage trocla/hiera integration

### <a name="trocla--params"></a>`trocla::params`

A set of default params for the main class

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `trocla::params` class:

* [`sysdomain_profile_name`](#-trocla--params--sysdomain_profile_name)

##### <a name="-trocla--params--sysdomain_profile_name"></a>`sysdomain_profile_name`

Data type: `String`

Name given to the default trocla profile.

Default value: `'sysdomain_nc'`

### <a name="trocla--yaml"></a>`trocla::yaml`

This will install and configure trocla with the
default yaml storage.

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `trocla::yaml` class:

* [`manage_data_dir`](#-trocla--yaml--manage_data_dir)
* [`data_file`](#-trocla--yaml--data_file)
* [`edit_uid`](#-trocla--yaml--edit_uid)

##### <a name="-trocla--yaml--manage_data_dir"></a>`manage_data_dir`

Data type: `Boolean`

By default the puppet module will create and set permissions on the
directory where the storage file is placed. Set this to false to avoid
managing the directory.

Default value: `true`

##### <a name="-trocla--yaml--data_file"></a>`data_file`

Data type: `Stdlib::Absolutepath`

Absolute path to where to store the passwords. This should be managed using
the package. Default: /var/lib/trocla/trocla_data.yaml

Default value: `'/var/lib/trocla/trocla_data.yaml'`

##### <a name="-trocla--yaml--edit_uid"></a>`edit_uid`

Data type: `String`

Name of the system group assigned to the storage file

Default value: `'puppet'`

## Functions

### <a name="trocla"></a>`trocla`

Type: Ruby 3.x API

This will create or get a random password from the trocla storage.

Usage:

    $password_user1 = trocla(key,[format='plain'[,options={}]])

Means:

    $password_user1 = trocla('user1')

Create or get the plain text password for the key 'user1'

    $password_user2 = trocla('user2','mysql')

Create or get the mysql style sha1 hashed password.

    $options_user3 = { 'username' => 'user3' } # Due to a puppet bug
                                               # this needs to be assigned
                                               # like that.
    $password_user3 = trocla('user3','pgsql', $options_user3)

Options can also be passed as a yaml string:

    $password_user3 = trocla('user3','pgsql', \"username: 'user3'\")

#### `trocla()`

This will create or get a random password from the trocla storage.

Usage:

    $password_user1 = trocla(key,[format='plain'[,options={}]])

Means:

    $password_user1 = trocla('user1')

Create or get the plain text password for the key 'user1'

    $password_user2 = trocla('user2','mysql')

Create or get the mysql style sha1 hashed password.

    $options_user3 = { 'username' => 'user3' } # Due to a puppet bug
                                               # this needs to be assigned
                                               # like that.
    $password_user3 = trocla('user3','pgsql', $options_user3)

Options can also be passed as a yaml string:

    $password_user3 = trocla('user3','pgsql', \"username: 'user3'\")

Returns: `Any`

### <a name="trocla--gsub"></a>`trocla::gsub`

Type: Ruby 4.x API

The `trocla_gsub` replaces %%TROCLA_[\w_\-]+%% place holders with
data looked up in trocla

#### `trocla::gsub(String $data, Optional[Struct[{ prefix => Optional[String] }]] $options)`

The `trocla_gsub` replaces %%TROCLA_[\w_\-]+%% place holders with
data looked up in trocla

Returns: `Any`

##### `data`

Data type: `String`



##### `options`

Data type: `Optional[Struct[{ prefix => Optional[String] }]]`



### <a name="trocla_get"></a>`trocla_get`

Type: Ruby 3.x API

This will only get an already stored password from the trocla storage.

Usage:

    $password_user1 = trocla_get(key,[format='plain'[,raise_error=true]])

Means:

    $password_user1 = trocla('user1')

Get the plain text password for the key 'user1'

    $password_user2 = trocla_get('user2','mysql')

Get the mysql style sha1 hashed password.

    $cert_x509_key = trocla_get('cert_x509','x509', 'render: keyonly')

Get the x509 style private key, by passing any trocla options as a third
argument.

By default puppet will raise a parse error if the password haven't yet been
stored in trocla. This can be turned off by setting false as a third argument:

    $password_user3 = trocla_get('user2','mysql',false)

or setting the 'raise_error' option to false:

    $password_user3 = trocla_get('user2','mysql', { raise_error => false })

the return value will be undef if the key & format pair is not found.

#### `trocla_get()`

This will only get an already stored password from the trocla storage.

Usage:

    $password_user1 = trocla_get(key,[format='plain'[,raise_error=true]])

Means:

    $password_user1 = trocla('user1')

Get the plain text password for the key 'user1'

    $password_user2 = trocla_get('user2','mysql')

Get the mysql style sha1 hashed password.

    $cert_x509_key = trocla_get('cert_x509','x509', 'render: keyonly')

Get the x509 style private key, by passing any trocla options as a third
argument.

By default puppet will raise a parse error if the password haven't yet been
stored in trocla. This can be turned off by setting false as a third argument:

    $password_user3 = trocla_get('user2','mysql',false)

or setting the 'raise_error' option to false:

    $password_user3 = trocla_get('user2','mysql', { raise_error => false })

the return value will be undef if the key & format pair is not found.

Returns: `Any`

The `trocla_lookup_key` is a hiera 5 `lookup_key` data provider function.

Returns: `Any`

##### `key`

Data type: `Variant[String, Numeric]`



##### `options`

Data type: `Hash`



##### `context`

Data type: `Puppet::LookupContext`



### <a name="trocla_set"></a>`trocla_set`

Type: Ruby 3.x API

This will set a password/hash in the local storage and return itself,
  or hashed in another format, if the password is present in plaintext or
  in that specific hash format.

  This function is mainly useful to migrate from hashes in manifests to trocla only manifests.

Usage:

    $password_user1 = trocla_set(key,value,[format='plain',[return_format,[options={}]]])

Means:

    $password_user1 = trocla_set('user1','mysecret')

Will set and return 'mysecret' as plain password.

    $password_user2 = trocla_set('user2','*AAA...','mysql')

Will set and return the sha1 hashed mysql password for the key user2.

    $password_user3 = trocla_set('user3','mysecret','plain','sha512crypt')

Will set 'mysecret' as plain password, but return a newly created sha512crypt hash.

    $postgres_user4 = { username => 'user4' }
    $password_user4 = trocla_set('user4','mysecret','plain','pgsql',$postgres_user4)

Will set the plain password 'mysecret' and return a pgsql md5 hash for user5.

    $password_user2 = trocla_set('user2','*AAA...','mysql','sha512crypt')

This will likely fail, except if you add the plain password or the sha512crypt hash manually to
trocla, for example via cli.

#### `trocla_set()`

This will set a password/hash in the local storage and return itself,
  or hashed in another format, if the password is present in plaintext or
  in that specific hash format.

  This function is mainly useful to migrate from hashes in manifests to trocla only manifests.

Usage:

    $password_user1 = trocla_set(key,value,[format='plain',[return_format,[options={}]]])

Means:

    $password_user1 = trocla_set('user1','mysecret')

Will set and return 'mysecret' as plain password.

    $password_user2 = trocla_set('user2','*AAA...','mysql')

Will set and return the sha1 hashed mysql password for the key user2.

    $password_user3 = trocla_set('user3','mysecret','plain','sha512crypt')

Will set 'mysecret' as plain password, but return a newly created sha512crypt hash.

    $postgres_user4 = { username => 'user4' }
    $password_user4 = trocla_set('user4','mysecret','plain','pgsql',$postgres_user4)

Will set the plain password 'mysecret' and return a pgsql md5 hash for user5.

    $password_user2 = trocla_set('user2','*AAA...','mysql','sha512crypt')

This will likely fail, except if you add the plain password or the sha512crypt hash manually to
trocla, for example via cli.

Returns: `Any`
